sprint: Gate Segment 2's lumoFlying Beat Now Loops for the Whole Flight
task: Direct follow-up: 'is the audio not in loop on second fly of lumo?' -- confirmed it wasn't. The lumoFlying clip (~2.7s) played once while Segment 2's own flight footage runs roughly 10s (from RESUME_AT_S to the video's real end), so the audio ended and went silent for the rest of the flight. js/lumoVoice.js gained an opts.loop parameter on play() (sets audio.loop before playing, every other caller unaffected since it's optional) and a new stop() function (pauses, resets currentTime, clears loop) -- exported on the LumoVoice public object. js/gatewaySequence.js's Segment 2 start now calls LumoVoice.play('lumoFlying',{loop:true}), stopped the instant playVideoSegmentToEnd's own onDone fires (right before the final flash); a matching LumoVoice.stop('lumoFlying') safety net was added both before the Segment 1 landing beat (in case that flight's own shorter, one-shot clip is still trailing) and inside clearTimers() (the one centralized cleanup point a mid-flight tap-to-skip already routes through), so a looping clip can never keep playing after the Gateway hands off. Verified via Playwright wrapping the real LumoVoice.play/stop functions (not a full mock) across the whole Traveller greeting cycle: the second lumoFlying call is confirmed to carry loop:true, and a matching stop('lumoFlying') call is confirmed to fire afterward. Full regression across the existing sky-challenge Playwright suite and both goldenBuild.js suites (World Builder v1 and v2) passes unchanged.
